text: If I could only not give a star. I bought a phone from tmobile paid in full. I no longer use the phone I got another one and I'm satisfied with it. So now I want to sell my old phone to someone because tmobile is offering cents nothing near to the 699 I paid but guess what since I owe a past due balance on my new account they won't unlock my old phone that I paid in full for. Wth kind of mess is this? This is so wrong I paid this off and this has nothing to do with the price of tea in damn China. It's my phone and they're still claiming it. Basically holding my phone hostage from me. Horrible Horrible Service. This can't be right.

I bought a new phone and it was unlocked, however, it was "T-Mobile unlocked."  I use Verizon.  (I did not know when I bought the new phone that I was unable to use it on Verizon).  I read online that if you sign up for a T-Mobile account for two months, they will unlock your phone and you can then use it on Verizon.  I went to a T-Mobile store and signed up for an account.  They asked me for my Verizon account number and PIN.  (The reason I gave them my Verizon account information was so that I could keep my Verizon phone number). The PIN number I provided did not work.  But they went ahead and activated my new T-Mobile account.  I assumed that my phone number did not change and they did not tell me that it was changing.  As soon as I left the store, I made a phone call and the recipient asked why I was calling from a different phone number.  This is how I found out that T-Mobile had changed my phone number without telling me.

I walked back into the T-Mobile store and canceled my account.  I was assured that I would not be billed for the service.  Two months later, I received a bill from T-Mobile for $60.  I called T-Mobile customer service and told them what happened.  They advised that they were canceling my account and that I had a zero balance.  I asked for a confirmation number and they advised that they do not provide confirmation numbers for such purposes.  A month later, I received a bill from a collection agency for $48 for the outstanding T-Mobile balance.  I called the collection agency and they said I needed to contact T-Mobile.  T-Mobile advised that they needed my T-Mobile PIN to access my account.  I did not know my PIN because my account was active for only ten minutes.  They advised that I needed to go to a T-Mobile store for help. 

I have no desire to go to a T-Mobile store so I can be harassed by the employees into opening a T-Mobile account.  This situation should be resolved over the phone.  However, I contacted T-Mobile four times and they refused to rectify the problem.

Poor service area, RUDE customer service. 
Haven't been able to send or receive text for 3 days...but since it's a Family plan only one person can contact customer service. 
It's BS...I am the one having problems,  why should 2 people have to be inconvenienced cause of it.
They definitely don't have thier s**t together. One rep will tell you one thing,  while another one says something else.

After the phone I bought stopped working after six months went in to a t-mobile store and was treated super rude. After trying to ask a question I was informed by an imployee that my wife was a b!tch and than a fu(k!n btch. After getting mad and being besides myself that this happened this same employee attacked me with a razor blade and than tried to hit me with a metal rod. It’s not even safe to be with t-mobile. Go to Verizon. We switched and the service is impecable and they don’t attack you with deadly weapons

Dysfunctional  charger & horrible customer service 
Not a trustworthy store
They're charging me and invoicing after I canceled already.

Switched 4 weeks ago to them and regret it. Since the beginning: Cannot register and/or login to their website or app- Text messages not working. Receive them only periodically. Phone calls getting straight to the voice mail. Called their customer service 8 times. Every time: "uh ah, I do not see a ticket. I will open one and tell our EXPERTS (looooool) to fix this right away. Please, please wait three days for my call"
Guess what? Never received calls. Switched to another carrier. Not worth the time to spend with them. It is embarrassing for a German company.

A customer service representative told me if I pay a little towards my bill there would be no service interruption but they shut my phone down and I called and asked them about it and they told me that the rep lied just to get some of the money now they want $100 plus reconnect fee's horrible company I'd rather pay more and just stayed with Verizon TMobile will never get any business from my family or friends they lie and steal

T-Mobile is horrible. Service was poor and phones iPhones did not work properly. Company never sent cards to pay off Sprint after repeated attempts and phone calls. I couldn’t give the company my business after all they didn’t keep their word. Now I have to pay two companies, but it’s worth it to get rid of T-mobile.

Terrible service calls going straight to voicemail no missed calls showing in my phone. Text messages never came through or were delivered hours later. Refused to credit my account for poor service. Claimed I didn't say anything  to them. Not true! Terrible. Switched companies.

I made the switch from Verizon to T-Mobile as to lower my bill. T-Mobile had wonderful customer service starting with my initial phone call. Got me set up and shipped everything to me, was hassle free. Due to life circumstances I had to move pretty quickly after starting my services, no problem! Except I had no service where I now lived, not even a peep. So I called, was again treated with wonderful service and they sent me a zone expander. It arrived in a timely fashion, and was easy to set up. However it didn't work for my area, so still had no service. We are talking over a month of not being able to use to the service I was actively paying for. So I call again, lady is super nice and says, I can return all the equipment, and be compensated for the month I didn't have service. FANTASTIC! I really felt like they wanted to help! Although that meant I was leaving their services, I still highly recommended them to anybody who lived in town, and could use the services. Fast forward a few months I'm back with Verizon when I get a phone call from a collection agency saying I owe my final bill, before it hits my credit. (The one they were supposed to comp since I don't have service) 
No biggy must of been a mistake I'll call them and get it all sorted. 20+ phone calls later I get told that they can't pay it as it's no longer held with T-Mobile and is now in the collection agencies hands. WHAT!? You mean to tell me you never once call about a balance? Send a bill? An email? Then send it to collections, even though you said you'd pay it!? Not a single person since this incedey has been in any way helpful, all basically saying too bad so sad. Where is the follow through? The accountability? Anything? It really sucks because I did enjoy the customer service at first and had recommended them so highly. I had 3 of my friends switch over within 3 months. Never again T-Mobile, never again!
its funny how they can just steal from month after month and when you bring it up nobody can help you it looks like they need an extra $16.00 dollars a month people make sure you look at your bill

Ever wonder why T Mobile gives TV's away for FREE,Well here ya go,WHEN SERVICE IS THIS BAD YOU'LL UNDERSTAND WHY...     If I could leave a minus star i would,I was with T Mobile 4 days and could not use my phone 1 day.I went back to Verizon and T Mobile sent me 2 bills 1 for $56 and 1 for $80 and they sent me to pre collections after several days and hours on the phone with sarcastic and argumentative customer representatives that promised they fixed the problem only to call back in a few days later to be back in the same situation all over again.I even spoke with supervisors that promised the situation was resolved,whats crazier even yet is i never got the $53.50 in a check or prepaid visa card as promised for the 2 SIM cards that were returned...I was told my Verizon SIM cards were destroyed but i would have to return the T Mobile SIMS that i used 4 days so i returned them and still NOTHING but weekly headaches.
the worst company in the world

This is a scam company. network coverage is terrible, the network works well only in the city, if you travel a long way away, change your carrier. I switched to another telecoms operator and saved my number, so Mobil drove me into debt like for another month of use ($ 75), and not talking about it! If I knew this, then I would not have left this phone number for myself. Very expensive internet and very few gigabytes.

They said I never paid my August bill, it's December now, even though I did. I was caught up on all of my bills and I doubt that T-Mo would let me keep my service going for this long. I just wish they would have called me or texted me saying I owed for a bill. This is ridiculous.

I signed up with T-Mobile (5 lines) around August of 2018. After realizing that the service wasn't great where I live, I ported four of my numbers to AT&T then canceled one.  At that point, I expected to receive a final bill and settle everything. 

In late November I notice that my card was still being charged so I called them up. I was on the phone for an hour before being told that the line would  be canceled and I would be refunded the almost $500.00 I was charged. 

After a month or so, I noticed that they only refund me $100.00. Upon calling back, the call taker was absolutely horrible. She told me that there was still an active line on my account and initially claimed that there was not record of me calling to cancel. After going back and fort for a while, she miraculously found that record of me calling to end service a month prior. She then insist that I would need to pay more money to T-Mobile and even went as far as saying that I still owed them money for the current bill. 

In all, they charged me around $535.00 after I ported out my four numbers and canceled my single line. I WILL NEVER DO BUSINESS WITH THIS COMPANY AGAIN!  Let this be a warning to all T-Mobile customer: DON'T GIVE THEM ACCESS TO YOU CREDIT/DEBIT CARD! THEY WILL STEAL YOUR MONEY AND GIVE YOU THE RUN-AROUND TO GET IT BACK!

So sick of t-mobile 
Service here in Manchester Deming Road, CT
needs improvement. For one, they need more sales associates .
Also chairs for customers to sit, absolutely unacceptable. This guy had to lean on the counter, due to no seating. Heads up, when you come Bring a chair, and pack a lunch the turn around time here is super long. They have no sign in sheet so stay woke because someone might jump a head of you in line. Just one ☝️ Person’s opinion. Manchester CT

My name is Linda, I have been a customer of T-Mobile for years. And right now, I'm furious with the company services. I was mislead by a store rep. I was told by a rep that I was eligible for a new free phone. I explained nothing is free. He stated that I was customer that have paid on time for 10 years. I accepted the purchase over a two years payments. Not! I have just been informed of some disturbing options that had nothing to do with my two years payments. T-Mobile has trained their reps to lie to get customers to pay for something they don't want. This is not acceptable. And someone will be held accountable, for this sort of practice. Do not go to this company for cell phone service. I will be filing this report to BBB.

they have to be the most incompetent people you could deal with,,we changed to T mobile is junk,,they gave us new phones,mine has never worked have fought with them for on and off for 3 years over the defective non working phone,that they continue to bill us for,phone will only work with wi fi we ave told them this over and over,they keep saying they will send a new one has never came,and every time we call they keep saying we will send it this time,,3 years at 50 bucks a month,around 1800 dollars for a phone line that does not work,,my advice would be a string between 2 dixie cups,much more reliable,then these crooks

I’m just glad I didn’t buy any new phones with them.

No stars, I hate T-Mobile.  I’m on hold right now and i’m going on 20 minutes.  They charge you $25 for a SIM card plus $25 more for activation.  There billing start on the 6th of every month so if you start on the 3rd then they charge you a full month from the 3 to the 5th.  Going back to AT&T

I have been a loyal customer for two years and when my note 5 stopped working they gave me the run around on replacing my phone. And may I add that I haven't had a phone in two months but I still paid my bill and still couldn't get a replacement phone they kept telling me I had to upgrade my phone which would require me to give them 328.00. then when I went to the store the associate there kept saying that it was time for him to go home like he was doing us a favor, when we are the customer. So I just called and cancelled two lines that I have with you guys. You have just lost a customer and I will never recommend this service to anyone

I feel T Mobile is out to scam their customers. They say one thing, then find an excuse to get out of it. I took my phone in for a 'jump on demand' to another phone, and after I peel off my screen protector and take my 360 wrap case off, clean it, I hand over my phone. They come back five minutes later saying the screen is cracked and they can't take it. I ask where. They show me a scratch I hadn't noticed on the forward facing camera lens. They say 'scratch here with your nail, you can feel it'. So I do, and I can barely feel a scratch but I see what they are talking about if I look close. But he insists the screen is CRACKED and not scratched. I have seen cracked screens. This is a scratch less than 2 millimeters long. Guess I'm finding another company. Their management is horrible. Rolando at Lakeline Mall, Cedar Park TX needs glasses. Lost my wife and I's account after this bull. ;

I switched from AT&T to T-Mobile after 3 different T-Mobile reps GUARANTEED me that I would be able to use my existing Samsung phones, do WiFi calling, and continue to download my contacts and calendar daily WITHOUT HAVING TO BUY ANY NEW PHONES.  
After spending hundreds of dollars on connection devices and SIM cards to switch and spending hours on the phone daily for days trying to make things work, they finally admitted that I would not be able to use my existing phones to do all of the above and I would have to buy  T-Mobile Samsung phones if I wanted all the functions to work properly.  It was a classic bait and switch.  They waited until I had switched to tell me I would have to spend $720 each for new T-Mobile Samsung phones to make everything work properly, knowing that it will now cost me another $100 to switch back to AT&T.

T-Mobile to anyone, period.

TMobile vs Straight talk 
So not to long ago I switched from straight talk to TMobile. I expected to have a better overall plan, reasonably priced, and to have faster LTE speeds. About a month into using TMobile I have really not noticed a change, in fact the speeds were anything but fast. I have lost service all together many times,it has reverted back to 3G a few times, more often then straight ever did. Their 4G must not have much coverage or something and lacks the performance I would expect from a higher end phone company. I compared it to my straight talk account (it's still active because the plan was paid for when I switched)  and I did a speed test for mps. Straight talk speeds were significantly higher compared to TMobile. In fact they were double the speed. I wanted to compare playing a 1080p video running on just mobile data. TMobile had a hard time loading the video as it buffered several time throughout the video. While straight talk on the other hand had no problems playing the high frame rated video,it didn't buffer or cut out once. Now as far as plans go, I  am on TMobile one plus, Supposedly I get 2x higher speed LTE over the other plans. Now it is a great deal or all it offers,20mps in flight LTE, I can text and what not in other countries, and and if I add another line they pay for Netflix.  But the plan is only $75 and I pay $90. What are the $15 in fees for? What are the fees? Straight talk offers unlimited high speed LTE, talk and text for only $55 and only has about $5 in fees. Ithink it's safe to say (for me anyway) TMobile still has a ways to go before their service is better. I think I'll stick with straight talk for the moment.
I am aware that large corporations like T-Mobile get a lot a bad press but my experience with T-Mobile has been satisfactory so far.

Tmobile is garbage! People are super unprofessional. Dont know what there talking about. YOU WILL ALWAYS GET AN EXCUSE FOR A PROBLEM YOU ARE HAVING. They almost NEVER get your problem resolved. Sick of this service, will be taking my business and money elsewhere. Do not recommened them in the least bit, not even if they were the last choice.

I bought a T-Mobile tourist SIM card while staying in the US for an extended period of time. My data didn't work at all or barely sometimes and I was staying in larger metropolitan areas all the time. It's worked before when buying data from my own operator in the US so it can't be the phone.

We were with T-Mobile for many years. Paid our bill on time ALWAYS. We moved to a town we’re we were in roaming with T-Mobile, so we had to switch carriers. Once we switched, both phones locked. We were in the middle of buying and selling a home and my husband getting a new job. We needed our phones to work. We contacted T-Mobile to unlock them and they said they would in 24 hrs. 24 hrs later, nothing. Called back. They said it would be 48 more hrs. 48 hrs, nothing. Called back. They said 72 hrs. 72 hrs. Nothing. We called back and they said they never had record of us calling. ?!?! We had gone down to T-Mobile, on the phone with them for hrs and still nothing was being done. We were even hung up on after being on hold for and hour and 45 minutes. After about a week and a half we finally got to someone higher up and he said there was nothing they could do because we had a balance on our account?? We said what is the balance, they wouldn’t tell us. We said we would pay it in full, right then and there, if they would just tell us the balance. Finally, after about and hour, they told us the balance, but said we had to wait until the next billing cycle. Then, once paid off, they would unlock our phones after 72 hours. It was a huge mind game. All they were doing was giving us the runaround. We were longtime customers, loved the service, always paid ON TIME, and just because we had to switch carriers because we moved to a place that T-Mobile didn’t cover, we we’re being punished and treated like scum. This caused us so much lossed time and anxiety. This literally made me sick! I don’t understand how a company can do something like this and get away with it. Very unprofessional. :(

Awful. Dishonest. 
Trying to by a house and when my lender ran my credit a charge from T-Mobile came up. I paid it right away. Made the mistake and didn’t get a deletion letter. I called T-Mobile begged and pleaded for once they told me to contact the credit company. I did the credit company told me to call T-Mobile went back and fourth for a week with them. Finally got a hold of some one who claimed they could help, they told me the letter was waiting for me at my closet T-Mobile store. I was beyond excited because this is my ticket to buying my house, got to the store and what do you know they had no idea what I was talking about and no proof of this letter. I know I did wrong by letting this go to collections but it was reported to my credit on October 23 and I saw it on November 13th and paid it in full right than and there. I know they are able to provide me with a deletion letter, they as a company is just not doing so. Because of this debit I now will be moving my family into a relatives house until this will fall off in 7yrs 😢 a 400 dollar debit stoped me from my dreams, all T-Mobile had to do was give me permission to have it removed. Oh and hey next time T-Mobile don’t lie to someone just to get them off the phone. I looked dumb walking into the store looking for my letter. Just cried the whole way home. Awful company.

T-mobile is a joke, I have spent $25,000 in merchandise on 7 years. I pay over $300 a month in phone bills. You all charged me for a year for a phone I never even had. Than didn't want to give me my money back. Now I take off a phone and a tablet and my bill is still  $256.07 that's a joke. I'm sure another phone company like Verizon wouldn't mind having my business.

Worst customer service and app!!!! I'm a T mobile user and I am very disappointed with the app and the service. We have called to customer service 4 times to set up our online account to pay and we always receive the same answer. "We will prioritize you and restart your account in 48 hours" but nothing happens. It is very difficult to online pay and the service is the worst. I will just change to another provider!

Bought sim card whilst visiting USA and paid extra for coverage in Canada.  Once outside NY I had no coverage and no data, called customer service who asked me to change settings on my phone, but they cared so little they didn't even call back as promised. Total waste your money, other than in NY, so frustrating when you pay for something that's unusable and they don't even care.    I was travelling with no navigation, no translator and no phone calls, just appalling.

T-Mobile has the worst customer service I have encountered in a long time.  I spent over 3 hours over a 2 day period trying to get a problem rectified and still have not been able to.  Still working on it.  You wait on hold for over 40 minutes just to speak to someone and then they say they will call you back and never do.

Do not order any accessories  from t mobile and return they will continue to bill you after you return and you will spend next 3 months to get it off your bill.Even if you have documents to prove  you sent it back.

This is my first experience with t mobile.   It was terrible. I went to the store for a prepay SIM.   The whole transaction took 30 minutes.   It did not activate. I was still in the store 3 hours later with no service. The sales rep was on hold with customer care. They never answered.   It was 8 pm and they sent me home with no service.  Closed the store and went home. I was back the next day and waited another 3 hours.  All they ended up doing was changing the phone number
  The reps must be on commission. Scoped out every  one who came in for a sale but let me sit for hours.  Poor poor service. Luckily I am using them for 30 days

I have been with Tmobile since 2002. They used to be an amazing company. But now it seems harder and harder to get things dropped off your account (FamilyWhere) without calling into Customer Service (in which you now have to have a long pin number - confirming information is no longer enough) or go into a store. You can no longer login and drop unwanted/unused items such as FamilyWhere. I am a busy person. I don't have time to sit around a store for an hour to an hour and a half till I am helped ... meanwhile I have been paying for something I don't even use. Unlimited data? I have their best all inclusive plan. 80% of the time my HotSpot does not work where I live. I have 3 bars at best. I lose every call placed 2 miles from the house in all directions. I am 10 miles from their closest store outside of the Austin area. Really not happy at what is being provided... Seriously think it's time for a change in cellphone providers!!

WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE ON THE PLANET!!! AVOID AVOID AVOID! Customer service wait time is obscene .. I'm currently waiting for 6.5 HOURS!!! Reps that you message with over the website have literally no ability to do anything except for refer you to the call center. DO NOT GET T MOBILE. Horrible coverage, absolutely insane customer service and waste of money!!!!!!!

WORST COSTUMER SERVICE EVER!!! Please, don't choose t-mobile if you have other choices. If you're out of luck getting no service/data, do not expect to get any refund from them. All they do is transfer your call and make you repeat the whole story again and again and get exhausted, and eventually gave up asking for any refund. I've called at least four times and waited for nearly two months. I received nothing after all.

Long story short....NO WAY GOING TO BE A CUSTOMER OF A COMPANY THAT CANNOT GET NEW CUSTOMER SERVICE CORRECT....PHONE BEING RETURNED AND BE GLAD TO PAY THE $50.00 RESTOCKING FEE TO NOT BE THEIR CUSTOMER...LESSON LEARNED.
Worst customer experience I have ever had.  I will avoid this company in future at all costs

I have been a customer with T-mobile for over 5 years now. I am extremely upset, hints why I’m going out the way to write a review. Back in April they had a promotion deal buy one get one iPhone 8 free. The catch was to have a second line transferred from another company. Being that my husband was with Verizon we took this opportunity to both join on my T-Mobile account. After signing up we received a MasterCard in the mail to pay off one iPhone. I called T-Mobile to specifically pay off the iPhone the same day I received it in the mail. The representative made Me believe she applied the $700 towards our device. Here we are in November I see my bill is higher then usual and I call them to figure out the issue and I was told the $700 was applied to my account and not the iphone. They will promote a BOGO and screw you over to have the rebate paid towards your account and NOT the other phone. DO NOT FALL FOR THIS PROMOTION. IT IS A FRAUDULENT PROMOTION DEAL. I will be sure to cancel me and my husband services with T-Mobile after them screwing us over like this.

About 8 months ago and probably even more, I was having problems with my phone line. I was calling in multiple times and letting them know that I wasn't getting any services, my calls were getting dropped and my text messages were coming in late. They supposedly troubleshooted it and it was supposed to work now. Well surprise, it was still NOT working. I kept calling back and paying my phone bill every month. Finally they said to get a new phone and it was the phone, so I went in and got a new phone. Surprise! AGAIN it still was not working. I got upset and didn't pay my phone bill. They suspended my line and when I called in, they not only charged me a late fee for EACH line but refused to hear me out or try to resolve my problem. I called back and their solution this time was that, "my lines were under verizon and that's why it wasn't working and now it is under Tmobile and should work now... and it STILL was not working properly. Now, I finally said screw it, cancel my lines, I will go else where and then they apologized and said they would take care of my bill and the late fees that they kept stacking on my account. I wasn't going to keep paying for services I was not getting and so the customer service rep for the "LOYAL" team reached out and said they would pay for my bill. She promised to call two days later to see how my service was working and to proceed with the promise. Two days later, I never got a response or a call. I called them and all of a sudden, that promise wasn't noted. Mind you when you call Tmobile, they state that all calls are recorded. I was transferred to 3 people, one of them a supervisor. She didn't care what I was going through and what I have had to deal with already, she just didn't care. Finally I told her to have the rep who promised me to call me. So we set up a time, they called me and asked for a specific appointment call and then I waited. I never got a call and about a day later, the rude supervisor called me back, apologized and said they would take care of the bill since it was promised. She said to give her about 2 to 3 days and she would clear my account. Well a week went by and the charges were still there. I called back today to talk to someone and they practically told me that they weren't doing anything and nothing was noted. When I asked to speak to the supervisor I talked to since everything was "noted", they rejected my request and said they couldn't do that and that their policy changed. I will be changing carriers and making sure to pass it along to friends, family, customers, clients, co-workers and everyone I know about Tmobile's service. I work in sales and now to be on the other end and get treated this way after PAYING money to have service and get told that I was practically "lying", I will never use them again.

They have absolutely no regard for people's time nor do they explain what the path to resolution is. I had to repeat myself 4 times to 3 different reps. I am not sure they even speak English. I wasted over an hour trying to get help and was passed around with no resolution what so ever. I will never spend money with this company and will be sure to let anyone who will listen know just how horrible the customer service is here.

I have spent more hours on the phone with T Mobile dealing with phone issues than I have actually spent using my phone in the last 3 months. 

They don’t care. It’s basically being transferred like hockey puck between associates, all of whom have one very specific specialty and none of which are actually helpful. 

I sincerely hope this company goes under. It’s the worst. 

I’m finding a new carrier ASAP. This has been my worst mobile phone experience and my worst customer service experience of my life.

Edited and rating raised ... a bit:  
 I filled out a customer service survey this morning for T-Mobile explaining what happened, as I did here.  Someone called and corrected the problem and lo an behold, they WERE able to send me new documents to the correct email address, contrary to what i was being told yesterday. 

However, I see that they street name on my account was wrong ... wrong street altogether ....  another stupid mistake by the same rep who added a totally wrong email address. 

Second error yesterday  ... the rep that I contacted to help me ... refused to help or refer my problem to someone who could help.. She argued with me instead.

But someone else noticed today  ... after I filled out their survey and it has been handled.  So I will change my rating to 2 stars ... from one, because good service after bad service is helpful, but it should not have happened in the first place.

Here's my original complaint:

My husband died so I thought I was doing the right thing, taking his phone, his ID, his death certificate and my phone and my ID in to the Palm Springs store to get everything in my name.  

They could not help me in the store,   but they  put me on the line to their customer service.  Since my husband was the primary account holder, not me ... they identified me, made a copy of the death certificate, closed his account and ran my credit to set up a new account for me.  But they could not have me sign the documents in the store (not something the store does, I guess) so told me they would email me the contract ... or whatever I needed to sign.    I was advised the it was imperative that I sign and return them via email, within 2 days or my account would be suspended.

I called when I did not get the documents and they were very busy, so the rep said someone would call me back within an hour.   UH ... 4 hours later ... no call.  So ....  I call again.

It seems that  they sent the documents to  a completely wrong email ... to I cloud (neither I nor my husband ever had an i cloud email).  NOW ... they tell me they cannot send it again until I go back in to the store and identify myself (I already did this once) ... and that they have to start all over.  

Not only do I not have the time to do this within the 2 day period when the store is open .... it is  their error ... not mine.  It took over an hour to get this done today, as it is.  I don't have another hour tomorrow ... during business hours.

I asked for a supervisor  to see if I could get a simple replacement email .... but she just argued with me and said that she could do nothing to help me .. no exceptions.  I told her I had already been identified in the store, when they closed the other account and opened mine.  

 So ... It seems that they are going to disconnect me ... since I have not signed their contract that they could not get emailed to me.   

No compassion for a grieving widow and , no way to fix their own error.   And then they will cut off my service ... when I desperately need it.

How can that be called "customer service"?   It's no service at all ....

T-Mobile has the worst service ever my fiance has been a loyal customer ever since he's come to the states we had just asked for an extension probably just for a day extension but the supervisor said she can do anything about it... As I was talking to her and explaining she put me on mute and then she just continued what she was trying to say but she wasn't listening to me I do not remember her name but I did call today and had the worst service of my life we've had this issue with T-Mobile and after having other problems with other services we came back to T-Mobile thinking okay we won't have issues this time but we're back to the same old thing. We have decided we are going to be disconnecting the service soon so if you guys want to think of a phone to go to don't pick T-Mobile the worst service ever. If any of the T-Mobile manager are reading this I hope you eat this really well because the supervisors you have they do not listen cuz if you guys have good customer service they would listen and they would try their best you guys are just losing a loyal customer of yours.

OK, I've been with T-Mobile now for a little over one year.

It has been a nightmare.

First, they told me that my Verizon Smartwatch would work great on their service.

After, AFTER, I switched over they told me it wasn't compatible.

Second, they showed me on their screen that I had their best service, download-wise, and that the checkmarks on the screen showed that it was verified.

At first, I got download speeds of 3 mbps, not the "at least 25" that they said I would.

But then it started slowing down. I am now at 0.43 - 0.83 mbps !!!

I cannot hotspot Netflix to my TV. I am even having problems opening my Google Calendar at times.

The Reps and Techs on the phone sound wonderful. They are sooo nice, but then I realized that they are actors, not techs. Such performances!

They tell you that you are part of a team, but when you ask to talk to a team member, they can't seem to get them on the line.


So just today, I was informed by a rep named "Isaiah" that my hotspot on my phone was not capable of streaming Netflix, that "I was asking too much", and that I should find another internet source. And when I repeated that I was only getting 0.83 download speed on my cell phone, he said that the system was running perfectly.


They are Con Artists.


I am now forced to go back to watching DVD's.


And I believe they are now trying to get rid of me by intentionally slowing down my speeds because I have been giving them bad reviews on their surveys. - One way to get a 100% satisfaction is to get rid of those that are not satisfied.


Oh, and just a note.

The other day I was at my local T-Mobile store. I checked my speed while I was in the store, 75 mbps download speed !!! BUT ! I walked out to my car in their parking lot, checked my speed again... 3 mbps.

Nice to have a dedicated line in the store.

I've been a loyal tmobile customer for 5 years and haven't had any problems until recently when we switched to the military One plan under my dad. Since then, we've had nothing but problems with everything from billing to no one being able to figure out how to put us on the correct plan. Every time we call in, we are told something different  and now tmobile blames us for issues they caused! I was told that I needed to turn my S7 Edge in or pay for it, so I turned it in and got a new lease under my dad's plan. I was then given a credit for the S7 lease of nearly $400, but now they are charging me that $400 and threatening to take me to collections if I dont pay it. Nobody seems to know what they are talking about, they apply credits and then take them back, put us on the 7 line plan when we only have 5 numbers, upgraded the plan to the more expensive base plan when I specifically said we didnt want that plan...it has been a NIGHTMARE!!!!  Then the retention department wants to make "it right" by just saying "sorry you were told wrong the previous 7 times you called in."
Tmobile should be ashamed of itself for treating our military and veterans this way! This is the WORST customer service I have ever recieved of ANY company I've ever done business with!

T-Mobile does a hard Credit Inquiry when you sign up with them which lowers your Credit Score dramatically! A Hard Inquiry is when you are looking to borrow money. The Inquiry stays with your Credit History forever! It may not appear on your Credit Report after 2 years, that is, unless they decide to do another one on you, which may happen every 12 months. I had an excellent Credit Score that went down the tubes with their Hard Credit Inquiry. Yet, I was not asking for any credit from them. allowed them to do automatic deductions from my credit card for my monthly bill which by the way your bill is one month in advance every month. Where is their risk? I am paying up front and I buy my phones direct from Apple. So, by signing up with them you will be lowering your Credit Score! When questioned they said that I agreed to let them do A Hard Inquiry. It was in the fine print that we all spend a few hours reading or having our Attorney review before signing! Right! I am dumping T-Mobiole before they do any more damage to my Credit History! This was a huge mistake! Should have stayed with AT&T. Better coverage and no Credit Checks!

Switched from AT&T to T-Mobile in June 2018.  Stay away from T-Mobile if you can!  Surely, the service is cheaper, but you get what you pay for:

Coverage: the service coverage in the San Francisco Bay Area is the worst I have seen - in comparison with AT&T and Verizon that I have used before.  I get one bar in most places and no coverage at home (Cole Valley).  T-Mobile suggested a range extender for my home, which I tried, but it doesn’t help at all.

Unlimited Data: since you barely ever get more than one bar in most places, guess what, you don’t use much data.  I find myself looking for free wifi whenever I need data.  My monthly data usage is about 1/20th of what I used to consume with other service providers like AT&T or Verizon.  So, sure, free unlimited data but very limited by poor coverage.


Phone battery drain: my phone keeps switching antennas.  In a number of places, one bar becomes two bars, then becomes No Service, then becomes one bar again.  This drains the battery very quickly.


Scam calls: the day I switched to T-Mobile, I started getting unwanted scam phone calls.  On average I get 15 to 20 such calls per day.  Each call is a nuisance, generates a voicemail and generates a text message from T-Mobile that usually reads something like: “A caller left a message that could not be reliably transcribed”.  Thanks for nothing I guess.  It is obvious that T-Mobile is not protecting its customers phone numbers.


Account hacks: since your phone number becomes pretty much public once yo become a T-Mobile customer, you are going to be a target for folks who hack into accounts that use your phone number for sign-ons.  Both my Lyft and Postmates account have been hacked into and I encountered $600 worth of fraudulent charges on Lyft and $200 on Postmates.  I have had to close both accounts and I am no longer able to use those apps.


Customer service: although the folks on the phone are friendly, the company is not taking any responsibility for any of the above.

so, i called t-mobile and talked to Erik. he said its regular charge and they can not do anything about it. so, i paid roughly $250 for terrible service with T-mobile!

so, after putting me on hold and again telling me that i was charged correctly and thats how it is, i reminded Erik that actually they have "Buyers Remorse period for 15 days". he claimed that its for devices only! then again, i told him that i have in writing from another customer service specialist that exactly "I" was under that period with my case.

after placing me on another hold, he came back with idea that - yes, i was under buyers remorse period and they will charge me only for period when service was activated, which were 11 days.

if thats true and they will return $96, then a week of terrible service and customer support and all inconveniences would cost me $150 with t-mobile.



my advice: STAY AWAY FROM T-MOBILE at any cost. they are liars!

We were T-Mobile's customers for 14 years! That's right. 14 years. We weren't under any contract, nor were we satisfied with their customer service, we were just too busy to switch. In June of this year, we moved to Europe. We weren't under any kind of contract with T-Mobile, but we notified them a month in advance that we are leaving the country. They said, no problem, we have to bill you in advance,because of our billing cycle, but then we will refund you. Instead of them refunding us for a month, they made up a debt of 2.5 times our monthly bill and sent it to collections. My husband even talked to them twice on the phone, and both times they promised a refund, but instead they sent this made-up sum to collections, potentially damaging our credit and wasting out time, not to mention that they still owe us money. They charged us for a month we cancelled and were in Europe not using their services.
We will definitely be using another provider when we move back to the States.

I purchased a router that was on sale through t-mobile.com.  2 weeks later I was wondering what the hell was taking so long, so I called them.  Apparently they cancelled the order without notifying me, because I did not answer a confirmation phone call they never told me was coming.  The CS rep on the phone told me they no longer carry the item, so I am out of luck.  I have never even heard of a company calling the customer to confirm a purchase, much less cancelling the order outright without notice because someone didn't answer.  The order was only 35 dollars, not kidding.  If they were going to call they could have just said at the time of my order, "You are going to get a call".  I am upset that 2 weeks later I do not have my router and have to go about paying more and waiting all over again to get it.  I feel that this company has cost me time, and time is money, so they have cost me money.  I feel they should have gone to extreme lengths to compensate me for their extremely poor handling of the matter.  There is absolutely no excuse for their behavior here.  I feel that this is a very poor company who does not give a damn about me or my business, and I will literally go out of my way to do everything I can over the course of my life from here on out to make sure that this company fails.  This company is pure garbage and should not be allowed to ever offer retail services in the United States.

Worst customer service.
Don't keep their promise.
Horrible. What a waste of time.

They dont know what they are doing... They are not trained properly. Eveytime u change something little on your account or add a line they mess it up and add additional services that are never agreed to. Then u get a bunch of charges on the bill. They say they did something u asked on the phone then u gotta call them 3 more times because they lied they actually dodnt fix anything. Had to call 4 times to change myself to a diff authorized user, everytime they said they did it. Had to call every month about my bill. Endless calls and waist of time. Dont do it people. Plus the customer service is not nice or apologetic at all. Most of them cant even speak english

I will save the details of many phone calls to the support that lasted on average 1+ hour and did not help at all.

I have an account in good standing, paid money, used my phone and then found that people cannot reach me. For 5 days I can make calls but cannot receive and have no data, the support answers like that:
1) You do not have an account (!!!)
2) Your account encountered an unusual error, we submitted a ticket, it will be resolved in 24 hours (that past long time ago)
3) Just getting disconnected after a while

I have never encountered worst customer service before. I will do my best to stay away.

It's time someone to tell about T-Mobile.  I bought 5 phones and service from T-Mobile.  One of the phones started giving a message "Not registered on Network" .  Took this back to the store and they said it's the sim card so they replaced the sim card and said if that doesn't fix it bring it back and we will do a warranty exchange.  Took it back and they said we will reset it and you can try again.  Wasn't it automatically reset when they replaced the card?  Again bring it back if it doesn't work.  Took it back has the software been updated?  Doesn't the phone get updated automatically?  It's up to date.  We will order a warranty replacement it will cost $5 for shipping.  Went to pick it up -- it's not in will have to re-order, so now it is in and go to pick it up.  You will need to pay for a new screen protector -- this is preposterous it's not my fault that the phone failed why should I have to pay for something that I already paid for?  Then the real dinger - it's 20 something dollars for the warranty exchange.  Am I being ripped off?  Is it just this store?  Where do I complain to T-Mobile?  I tried to ask this to online customer service -- get still trying to connect for more than an hour now?  I wouldn't recommend T-Mobile to anyone.  I had them before and thought they were ok but now I believe that they have expanded too quickly.  The guy at the store was rude so I returned it in kind.  The store is on Battlefield in Springfield, MO.
